reviews.review_description:
This remains my favorite VR game, years after I played it first on another platform. The free-ranging jumping gameplay takes full advantage of VR, and the movement mechanic is comfortable, since it's point-and-click (no arm swinging required). There's plenty of content too, with optional speed and collection challenges.

The weak points: some of the later mechanics (such as ball-in-socket) are a bit fiddly, and the graphics are plain, even by VR standards. But overall the gameplay here is great.

reviews.review_title: For those whose progress is not saved.
reviews.review_description:
The fix is to manually set permission for storage access. The game unfortunately doesn't display prompt asking for access. After setting up storage access permission the game saves progress correctly.
I already contacted developer, he promised to look at this, but I was quicker to find out :)


Can't praise enough how much I adore this game. It's like a dream of being able to fly. The game is all about parkour, but it gives a sense of freedom like a flight ( there is even a flight later in the game). With the coop addition it is even better. Best game on Quest of course!

So as someone else wrote, the storage access is OFF by default for the game. Go in the Quest parameters, privacy settings, look for the game and turn ON the storage access. Progression will then be saved. Tested myself on quest 3

Changing my review from one star to five, because IT FINALLY SAVES AGAIN ON QUEST 3! 

Here's the kicker, I don't know if they patched it, or if my reinstalling the gane popped the "Allow this game to save data to your device?" dialog and that did it, but either way I don't care, because TO THE TOP IS BACK!

So Electric Hat devs, if you patched it, thank you!

And if you're one of the many Quest 3 owners in the comments experiencing the same issue, I'd say try reinstalling and looking for that dialog. Good luck! 

Now if you'll excuse me, I'm off to bounce up the side of a giant robot.
I've got a room full of disappointed people. I hyped this as the best VR game and then 💥, it won't save our progress 📛🔥🔥👿😡

reviews.review_title: I AM BEGGING YOU TO FIX THE SAVE ON Q3
reviews.review_description:
PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE

I love this game absolutely to death and played through the whole first couple sections in one sitting and was like wow this game is so freaking fun but then to find out it’s glitched on Q3 and won’t save any progress rn made me so sad

I don’t care what you have to do please fix for Quest 3 or at least tell us how we can fix it, I love this game and want to be able to play it

reviews.review_title: Updated review. My favorite VR game
reviews.review_description:
Look through some of the other reviews if you are having problems saving.

This is my favorite VR experience and I just wish more people knew about it

reviews.review_title: Great game,but doesnt save on quest 3
reviews.review_description: I loved this game on quest 2 but when i play it on quest 3 the game save wouldnt transfer over and had to start again which wouldnt have been a problem  except it wont save at all.each time i play it i have to start over.i played it again on quest 2 and had no problems.hope this gets fixed

my og review was me saying that i didnt enjoy the game and wanted a refund, but upon further review i found it was actually really fun. The only problem now is that the game won't save my progress if the devs can tell me how to save it or change it so I can ill bump the stars up to 5

reviews.review_title: ALWAYS DESERVED TO BE ON QUEST STORE
reviews.review_description: The fact that this is an "applab" game is a JOKE. a 100% full, polished, amazing and fun game. Someone at oculus is a dunce for rejecting this from the main store.

This game is absolutely amazing! I was on the edge when buying it because the screenshots look pretty underwhelming and cluttery, but it turned out to be worth it. Phenomenal soundtrack, great level design and originality make it one of my favorite games on the Quest.

Read the reviews and thought this was the second coming of Jesus so I told myself I better get it. 

It is fun. But not what I thought it was going to be. I must be missing something or I haven't gotten deep enough into it to truly appreciate it. I'll revise my review as I play. But as of right now.. it's okay. 

Seems pretty hyped up so it must just be me.

To start, I have no idea why this is not on the official store. I have about 40 games on my quest, and I haven't played any of them since I got this one, a few days ago. It's polished, has nice graphics, and most important, it's a lot of fun. 
In terms of content, it features a single player campaign which basically has you race through levels, collect crystals and find difficult, hidden areas to get badges, that are used to unlock the next levels. It also features multiplayer, but I haven't tried that one yet.
It features over 30 levels that start out small and simple, but eventually get rather large, challenging, and stunningly beautiful (looking at you giant robot and honey level).
The controls take a bit of getting used to,  but after it clicks, it feels natural, and you really get the taste of being a super-hero robot from the future, climbing, jumping and gliding over trippy landscapes.
I felt almost no motion sickness, (except for a level that had me jumping on a rotating tower) but I did almost lose my balance once or twice while spinning around. I now play it on a swivel chair (I highly recommend trying it)
The only complaint I have with the game, is the sound. The songs are actually good, but there are too few of them, and they are also not thematically appropriate ( I would have preferred some instrumental rock/post-rock music, or futuristic synthwave/EDM with no lyrics). 
In conclusion, while 20$ might seem a lot for an app lab game, I feel the game offers more value than some of the 30$ games on the official store.

reviews.review_title: Super fun game with inventive levels
reviews.review_description:
I've had this game for awhile and it's been super fun, and I would have rated this a 5 stars. However, recently, every time I exit the game (from the main menu, too) the progress seems to reset, and the next time I come in, I have to start over from the tutorial again. This is very annoying as I don't want to complete the same levels over and over again without being able to do the more advanced ones! Please fix this and I'll bump it to the 5 star it should be.

Update: It turns out Cloud Sync was broken at the time, so it's not the game's fault. This is a fabulous game with really fun and inventive levels, highly recommended!
This is actually another climbing game but jumping is the main part of this game.

reviews.review_title: Best game on the quest imo
reviews.review_description: Why this isn't on the official app store is beyond me. The gameplay, the graphics , the controls and level design are freaking SICK. I recommend getting extremely elevated before playing this game (if ya catch my drift) ;) its amazing to say the least. Spent probably around $300 on quest games and this one takes the cake. 5/5 man. This is what vr is all about to me. If you have ever wondered what it would feel like to jump 10 + feet in the air from rooftop to rooftop then this is for you. Also there are some seriously wacky levels to jump through, gave me some Supermario Sunshine vibes ya know those special levels. Buy this game. Do it now. Do it for the children. Treat ya self.
The game is super fun once you get the hang of it and I played the same level 5 times without getting bored of it, plus the music that plays in the backround is amazing
They updated the game with the quickness so now I can say this game is one hundred present awesome and is a thrill to play. It’s like a mirrors edge vr. Love everything about this game!!!
cant wait for Super Kit (To The Top 2)

Top game 
I wasn’t expecting this game to be good like this but I realized I always go back to play.

Great game should be in the store
